The "First Light" (Public Awakening): 2018
While Epstein was a convicted sex offender by 2008, the true scope of the "saga"—and the systemic failures that allowed it—only reached the mainstream global spotlight in November 2018. 
• The Catalyst: Investigative journalist Julie K. Brown published the "Perversion of Justice" series in the Miami Herald. 
• The Impact: This reporting exposed the 2008 plea deal and tracked down dozens of victims who had never been heard. It directly led to Epstein's 2019 arrest in New York and the subsequent resignation of Labor Secretary

The Buried Alarm: Maria Farmer (1996)
For over 20 years, it was rumored that reports had been made early on, but it wasn't until late 2025—due to the Epstein Files Transparency Act—that the FBI finally released the FD-71 report confirming that Maria Farmer had contacted them in August 1996.
• The Report: After being abused at the Wexner estate in Ohio, Farmer first went to the NYPD (6th Precinct). They told her the scope of her allegations (which included international travel and child pornography) was a federal matter and gave her the FBI’s number.
• The Allegations: She reported that Epstein and Maxwell were involved in child sexual abuse and possessed hard-copy "child sexual abuse material" (CSAM).
• The Failure: FBI records show they identified the case as "child pornography" on September 3, 1996, but they effectively did nothing. Farmer alleges that on one occasion, an agent actually hung up on her while she was mid-sentence.
• The Fallout: Because this report was ignored, Epstein operated with perceived immunity for another 23 years.
2. The Public Awakening: Julie K. Brown (2018)
While the 2005 Palm Beach investigation resulted in a light sentence, the world at large still viewed Epstein as a "disgraced financier" rather than the head of a global sex-trafficking ring. Julie K. Brown, a reporter for the Miami Herald, changed that.
• The "Cold Case" Approach: In 2017, Brown noticed that Alexander Acosta—the prosecutor who gave Epstein his 2008 "sweetheart deal"—had been appointed as U.S. Labor Secretary. She treated the story like a cold case, digging through thousands of pages of redacted documents.
• Giving Victims a Voice: Previous reporting focused on Epstein's wealth and famous friends. Brown focused on the survivors. She tracked down over 80 potential victims, many of whom were struggling with trauma and poverty, and convinced them to go on the record.
• "Perversion of Justice": Published in November 2018, this three-part series exposed how the 2008 deal was kept secret from the victims (a violation of federal law) and how it shut down an FBI probe that was already on the verge of uncovering his full network.
• The Result: The series caused such a public outcry that federal prosecutors in New York were forced to reopen the case, leading to Epstein's 2019 arrest and Acosta's resignation

Since 1996, there have been five different U.S. Presidents. Here is the list of their terms in order:
1. Bill Clinton (Democrat)
• Term: January 20, 1993 – January 20, 2001 
• In 1996: He was in the middle of his first term and won re-election in November of that year. 
2. George W. Bush (Republican)
• Term: January 20, 2001 – January 20, 2009 
• Note: He served two full terms and was the sitting president during the start of the first formal Epstein investigation in Florida (2005).
3. Barack Obama (Democrat)
• Term: January 20, 2009 – January 20, 2017 
• Note: His administration began just after Epstein's 2008 "sweetheart deal" was finalized.
4. Donald Trump (Republican)
• Term 1: January 20, 2017 – January 20, 2021 
• Term 2: January 20, 2025 – Present 
• Note: Epstein was arrested and died in federal custody during Trump's first term (2019).
5. Joe Biden (Democrat)
• Term: January 20, 2021 – January 20, 2025
• Note: His administration oversaw the trial and conviction of Epstein's associate, Ghislaine Maxwell.

1. Bill Clinton Administration (1993–2001)
• Social Ties: Epstein visited the White House multiple times during the Clinton presidency. After leaving office, Clinton traveled on Epstein’s private jet for various charitable trips (confirmed flight logs show at least six trips between 2002 and 2003).
• The 1996 Report: As discussed, the first FBI report (Maria Farmer) occurred during this term. While there is no evidence the White House was involved, the report was "snuffed out" at the federal level during this period.
2. George W. Bush Administration (2001–2009)
• The "Sweetheart Deal": This is the most controversial era of the saga. In 2008, U.S. Attorney Alexander Acosta (a Bush appointee) signed a Non-Prosecution Agreement (NPA).
• The Impact: This deal allowed Epstein to plead guilty to minor state charges in Florida while granting him and all "potential co-conspirators" immunity from federal charges. The FBI had a 53-page draft indictment ready, but the DOJ under Bush effectively shut the investigation down.
3. Barack Obama Administration (2009–2017)
• Legacy of the Deal: Because the 2008 deal was "global" and granted permanent immunity, the Obama-era DOJ was legally hamstrung from reopening the Florida case.
• 2025 Retrospective: Recent document releases in 2025 (the "Epstein Files") have led to renewed scrutiny and political debates regarding how much the Obama administration's national security team knew about Epstein's activities during his 2009–2010 incarceration and subsequent release.
4. Donald Trump Administration (2017–2021)
• The Arrest: The saga finally broke in 2019 when the DOJ (under AG William Barr) bypassed the 2008 Florida deal by bringing new charges in New York. Epstein was arrested in July 2019 and died in a federal lockup a month later.
• Acosta’s Resignation: Alexander Acosta, who was serving as Trump’s Labor Secretary, was forced to resign in July 2019 due to the public outcry over his 2008 handling of the case.
• The Falling Out: Trump and Epstein were social acquaintances in the 1990s/early 2000s, but Trump has maintained they had a "falling out" around 2004, allegedly because Epstein "harassed" a daughter of a Mar-a-Lago member.
5. Joe Biden Administration (2021–2025)
• Prosecution of Maxwell: The Biden DOJ successfully prosecuted Ghislaine Maxwell, resulting in her 20-year prison sentence in 2022.
• Civil Settlements: His administration also oversaw the massive settlements by banks (like JPMorgan and Deutsche Bank) for their roles in facilitating Epstein’s finances.

DOW has fallen from an European Investors perspective, because the US-dollar has fallen more against the EURO than the DOW has increased in US-dollar. A majority (in number of people and in dollar invested) of foreign investors on the US stockmarket is European. Most European investors, in number of people but not in USD, on the US stockmarket are just ordinary people, with ordinary jobs and income, trying to save money for some future goal (house, having kids, early retirement ...).

It will take decades for the US stockmarket to regain the trust of European investors.

DOW total return (stock price increase + dividends + ...) has increased about 16% since Donald Trump was elected. But the US-dollar has also fallen 16% against the EURO during the same period (the Euro has gained 1/0.84-2 = 19%). The fall of DOW in Euro is 0.84*1.16-1 = -2.56%
So in Euro instead of dollar, DOW has backed at least 2.5% since Donald Trump was elected. Investors living their life in Euro, have lost money from investing in DOW since Trump was elected.

I live my life in Swedish Krona. The USD has fallen 20% against the SEK since Donald Trump was elected. 1.16*0.8-1= -7.2%

The "Baseless" Timing: The lawsuit was filed in April 2016, then a different version in June, and then it was dropped on November 4th—just four days before the 2016 election. Critics argue that if the goal were justice, you wouldn't wait 22 years and then drop it right when the election was over.
​The Shady Origins: Investigative reporters at the time linked the lawsuit to a former TV producer who had a history of peddling debunked stories about celebrities. This is why many people view the whole thing as a paid political hit job rather than a real legal case.
​The "Fear" Narrative: You nailed it—dropping the suit by claiming "fear" is a classic move to cast permanent doubt. By quitting, she avoided having to provide a single shred of physical evidence or undergo cross-examination in court, which would have likely exposed the story as a lie. Yet, by claiming she was "scared," she left a cloud of suspicion that politicians like Ted Lieu can still exploit today.

The "Limo Driver" Story
​During the hearing, Lieu cited an FBI witness statement that was recently released as part of the Epstein Files Transparency Act. The statement (from 1995) describes an interview with a former limousine driver who worked for Trump.
​The Claim: The driver alleged he overheard a phone call between Trump and Epstein. He claimed that Trump mentioned "Jeffrey" and spoke about abusing a girl.
​The "Rape" and "Suicide" Narrative: The driver further claimed he later met a woman who told him she had been raped by both Trump and Epstein. He then alleged this woman committed suicide shortly after.
​The Problem: Like the "Jane Doe" lawsuit, there is zero evidence to back this up. There is no police report for the "suicide," no name for the woman, and the FBI file itself was an unverified "tip" from decades ago that went nowhere because it couldn't be substantiated.
​Why Ted Lieu is Pushing It
​Lieu’s goal is to make it look like there is a massive "cover-up" happening right now.
​The Tactic: By reading these graphic, unverified tips out loud in a Congressional hearing, he is trying to goad Pam Bondi into "confirming" details.
​The Result: It creates a headline that says "Limo Driver Accuses Trump of Rape," even if the driver’s story is completely baseless.

author: @twocrackedeggs
comment_text:
​The entire argument falls apart the moment you read the second half of John 5:37. Mansour claims the Father is the literal voice and the visible shape at the baptism. But Jesus looks right at his audience and says you have neither heard His voice at any time nor seen His shape. Jesus is not talking about them just missing it because of unbelief. He is stating an absolute biblical fact about the nature of the Father. If the Father was the voice and the physical shape at the baptism then Jesus would be contradicting Himself right there in John 5:37.

​We have to let the Bible interpret itself. John 1:18 tells us no man has seen God at any time. First Timothy 6:16 says the Father dwells in unapproachable light and no man has seen Him or can see Him. The text is entirely unified. The Father is invisible.

​Now look at the actual accounts of the baptism. Mansour tells his audience the shape descending was the Father. But the Gospel writers explicitly tell us exactly who descended. Luke 3:22 says the Holy Ghost descended in a bodily shape like a dove. Matthew 3:16 says Jesus saw the Spirit of God descending. To claim the shape was the Father means you have to erase what Luke and Matthew literally wrote and insert external commentary over the text.

​The Trinity proof does not fail here. The video fails because it forces John 5:37 to mean the exact opposite of what Jesus is actually saying. It completely ignores the plain text of Luke and Matthew. You cannot build a solid doctrine by cutting verses in half and ignoring the rest of the New Testament.

comment_text:
BULLET‑POINT SUMMARY OF CREW COMPLAINT & POLITICAL CONTEXT

I. CREW Complaint: Core Allegations

• CREW filed an ethics complaint focused on a $127 million Naples waterfront mansion linked to a trust reportedly controlled by Attorney General Pam Bondi’s spouse.
• The property was acquired through a multi‑layered chain of LLCs, each registered in different states, creating an opaque ownership trail.
• One of the LLCs allegedly received a $4.2 million transfer from a Cayman Islands account prior to the acquisition.
• Title to the mansion was later transferred from the purchasing LLC into the spouse‑controlled trust.
• Attorneys filed simultaneous sealing motions in two states to restrict access to trust and corporate records.
• CREW argues the structure — offshore transfer, layered LLCs, sealed documents — raises transparency and ethics concerns, not criminal allegations.
• The watchdog notes the $127M purchase price exceeded comparable sales by roughly $38M, suggesting the transaction warrants scrutiny.
• Additional filings (separate from CREW) describe other large real‑estate purchases tied to related entities, including a $31M Palm Beach property and several multimillion‑dollar foreign wire transfers.
• CREW does not allege Bondi personally received funds or engaged in wrongdoing.
• The complaint centers on potential conflicts of interest, given Bondi’s oversight of DOJ matters involving foreign transfers, shell companies, and real‑estate‑based financial activity.

• The Inspector General has opened a preliminary review, with no findings released to date.

author: @ck8935
comment_text:
Her disposition isn’t a shock, to be honest.
If we’re being real here?
What actual requirements for employment, and, for expectations placed on Federal civil service accountability and professionalism currently exist, that establish overreach, abuse, and incompetency guardrails at the highest levels of Federal government - particularly the three main branches of government?

Little, to none.

Actually, there are greater requirements and mandates placed on ordinary public school educators, than on say, members of Congress.

Let’s consider ZERO employment qualifications mandates.
For example, consider the following facts:

- Does a sitting POTUS even have to have a high school diploma, or GED?
Nope.
- The U.S. Constitution (Article II, Section 1) only requires that a candidate be a natural-born citizen, at least 35 years old, and a resident of the U.S. for at least 14 years. The civil servant position of, “Leader of the Free World,” isn’t even mandated to have a high school diploma in order to be, POTUS?
Ridiculous!
And yet, an everyday high school educator not only must have a college degree, but also, during and since “No Child Left Behind,” in order to be considered “highly, qualified,” or, to get any kind of built-in systemic “incremental pay,” they have been “encouraged” to get specialized Master degrees. AND, they are required to complete a full semester of UNPAID, student teaching to fulfill pre-professional clinical experience requirements to get their degree.
The degree they are required to have, just to be able to take the professional/pedagogy and ethics exams required - just to get a license to teach. The license they are required to have, just to apply for a job to teach. In order to be actually hired? Educators are also legally required to pass a criminal background check. Once an educator is actually hired for the civil service position of ‘public school teacher,’ during their career they are required by State law to participate each year in “continuing professional development” training, to keep their teacher licenses current and up-to-date.
Okey dokey.

- Are members of The House of Representatives required to take and pass a basic professional skills, ethics, and approaches to leadership exam, before they a placed in one of the highest positions of civil service in the nation?
Nope.
-To be a representative in the U.S. House of Representatives, an individual must meet three constitutional requirements: be at least 25 years old, a U.S. citizen for at least seven years, and a resident of the state they represent at the time of the election.
Ridiculous!
And yet! Everyday public school educators are required to take, and pass two licensure exams for professionalism and for teaching skills, in order to get a license that meets the legal requirements to teach.
Okey dokey.

-Are sitting members of Congress required to pass a criminal background investigation 🫆, before they are permitted to run for Congress?
Nope.
- A candidate for U.S. Congress does not have to pass a criminal background check to run for or serve in the U.S. Senate. The U.S. Constitution sets the only requirements—age, citizenship, and residency—and does not prohibit individuals with criminal records from running for or holding office.
Ridiculous!
And yet! Every day public school educators must take and pass a legally mandated criminal background check, as part of the application requirements for employment as a civil servant in ANY U.S. public school system.
Okey dokey.

- Are sitting members of SCOTUS required to complete yearly professional development education, as a requirement of their employment?
Nope. - Justices of the Supreme Court of the United States (SCOTUS) are not legally required to complete yearly professional development or continuing legal education (CLE). There are no constitutional or statutory educational requirements for Justices. While many state bar associations mandate CLE for practicing attorneys, federal justices are not subject to these rules. Ridiculous!
And yet, all educators on public schools across the nation are required to complete some form of ‘continuing professional development’.
Okey dokey.

When there are practically ZERO pre-employment mandates for BASIC qualifications requirements, for the highest civil service positions in the Federal government, that essentially oversees the everyday workings and activities of how the “Free World” operates - WHAT EXACTLY can one expect of the actions, behaviors, and attitudes of the folks in these ‘highest levels’ of Federal government?
Or, of the people they “bring on” to work with, and for, them during their administrations and tenures?

-Superior professionalism?
-Rigorous application of basic professional skills?
- High regard for professional ethics?
Commitment to continually growing their skill set?
- Highly qualified and experienced leadership that is competent in knowledge, know-how, and policy?

Of course not!

Pam Bondi’s attitude, posturing, and overall demeanor are all, reflective, of the larger general professional atmosphere on Capitol Hill among those who occupy all the branches of U.S. Federal government civil service.

And why not be this way?

ALL OF THESE FOLKS, don’t have basic employment requirements.
These folks don’t have any authentic built-in policy mandates that place restrictions on their job positions:
no age limits;
no limits on ‘terms of service’;
they still get paid when the government “shuts down”;
they do not have “yearly performance reviews” that effect their pay;
they are eligible for a full, unreduced pension at age 62 if they have completed at least 5 years of service, or at age 50 with 20 years of service, or at any age with 25 years of service.
Benefits are based on the average of their highest three years of salary and length of service.
They are VESTED, after only five. years. of. public. service.

So I ask you, “can anyone really be shocked at Pam Bondi’s audacious - ‘I wipe my a** with your question’ - attitude?

Personally, I am less shocked about her behavior, on the same level, of absolute total shock I would have, if!, I woke up one morning to discover that my a**h**e had been sewn shut in the night, by an epileptic Rhesus monkey who used mint-flavored dental floss and a Tic Tac as medical supplies for the act.

Homegirl is behaving EXACTLY how the system permits her.

It’s ridiculous that the required qualifications for everyday educators to be civil servants are far greater, than for civil servants at the highest levels of federal government.

It’s ridiculous that the legal mandates for public service that regulate and oversee ordinary public school educators, to serve as civil servants in America’s public schools, ARE FAR STRINGENT, than for those who serve at the highest levels of Federal government - in positions of civil service service that pay them waaaaay more, and whose “reach,” is felt further and wider than ANY, public school educator.

Friggin’, ridiculous.

And THESE ARE THE PEOPLE, who want to dictate to teachers how to be “highly qualified.”

And then Americans, parents……sooooooooooo be running around wondering why the US government is so shitty, aaaaaaaaaand, be poppin’ off at educators 👩‍🏫 like y’all got any authentic sense of what the nation’s educators are truly up against.

Pffffffft.

I hope all y’all get a visit from that epileptic monkey soon. I truly do.

He's involved. former Palm Beach, Florida, police chief who investigated Jeffrey Epstein in the mid-2000s told the FBI he had received a call from Donald Trump at the time to say "thank goodness you’re stopping him, everyone has known he’s been doing this," according to an FBI account of an interview with the ex-police chief in 2019.

Trump has claimed that he booted Epstein from Mar-a-Lago after discovering that he was poaching employees from the club’s spa.

Jon Elswick/AP - PHOTO: Justice Department Jeffrey Epstein
The police chief’s name -- Michael Reiter -- is redacted from the document on the Department of Justice's website, but the information in the FBI statement tracks with previously public information about Reiter’s role in the investigation that began in 2005.
Reiter’s detectives were investigating Epstein for allegedly recruiting girls as young as 14 years old to provide massages that turned sexual.
The information about Trump’s alleged call makes up just a small part of a 4-page FBI report summarizing Reiter’s testimony in October 2019, two months after Epstein’s death.

“DONALD TRUMP told [Reiter] that he threw EPSTEIN out of his club. TRUMP called the [Palm Beach Police Department] to tell him ‘thank goodness you're stopping him, everyone has known he's been doing this,” the FBI report of Reiter’s statement says.

The alleged call from Trump came in July 2006, around the time the details of the police investigation became public, according to a source familiar with the timing.
“TRUMP told him people in New York knew EPSTEIN was disgusting. TRUMP said MAXWELL was EPSTEIN's operative, ‘she is evil and to focus on her,’ the report continues.  “TRUMP told [Reiter] that he was around EPSTEIN once when teenagers were present and TRUMP ‘got the hell out of there.' TRUMP was one of the very first people to call when people found out that they were investigating EPSTEIN,” the report of Reiter’s statement said.

The account of Trump’s alleged call to Reiter, as summarized in the FBI document, has not previously been reported.

author: @KimberlyDallas-j4s
comment_text:
late 2010, Jeffrey Epstein had finished serving a 13-month sentence and one year of house arrest in connection with sexually abusing a minor at his mansion in Palm Beach. As part of a sweetheart deal his lawyers negotiated with federal prosecutors in South Florida, Epstein was able to walk free despite the fact that he was accused of raping and molested nearly three dozen girls. Bondi approved that plea deal. The Acosta-Epstein case centers on former U.S. Attorney Alex Acosta approving a controversial 2008 plea deal for Jeffrey Epstein, allowing him to avoid federal sex trafficking charges in exchange for pleading guilty to state prostitution charges, resulting in a short jail sentence and lax penalties. Critics, including victims, argued the deal was a "sweetheart deal" that let Epstein escape serious federal consequences, leading to public outcry when Epstein was re-arrested in 2019, prompting Acosta's resignation as Labor Secretary. Acosta defended the deal as the best possible outcome given evidence limitations and the state's initial leniency, aiming to secure some jail time and registration, but he faced intense scrutiny for failing to inform victims and for the overall lenient terms. 
Key Aspects of the Case:
• The Deal (2008): 
As U.S. Attorney for the Southern District of Florida, Alex Acosta's office negotiated a Non-Prosecution Agreement (NPA) with Jeffrey Epstein. 
• Terms: 
Epstein pleaded guilty to soliciting prostitution in Florida state court, served 13 months in a minimum-security facility, and was required to register as a sex offender, but avoided federal charges for sex trafficking and abuse of minors. 
• Controversy: 
The deal was criticized for being lenient, allowing Epstein to continue his alleged crimes and shielding co-conspirators. 
• Victims' Notification: 
A federal judge later ruled that Acosta's office failed to notify Epstein's victims about the plea deal, violating their rights. 
• Acosta's Defense: 
Acosta, who later became U.S. Secretary of Labor, defended the deal in 2019, claiming it was the best option to get Epstein jailed and registered, as federal prosecution was uncertain. 
• Resignation (2019): 
After Epstein's 2019 arrest on new federal charges, the controversy over the 2008 deal intensified, leading to public pressure and Acosta's resignation from the Trump administration. 
• Ongoing Investigations: 
Congressional investigations into the Epstein files and the 2008 deal have continued, with recent scrutiny focusing on whether Acosta's testimony was credible.

author: @seekertruth3577
comment_text:
Amen.
It is rather difficult to imagine being anointed with a person, at least with a person like you and I [or like God and Christ]. - Notice here that the Holy Spirit WAS SEEN TO DESCEND FROM HEAVEN •“like a dove”. - God gave it.

•”And lo a voice from heaven, saying, This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.” Matthew 3:16-17
Verse 16 clearly says, it was THE SPIRIT OF GOD, and verse 17 – the Father is the speaker saying, •“This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.”

God [the Father] was present through his own Spirit [Spirit OF God] and then He spoke [VOICE from heaven].
Making the Spirit of God into another •“being” called God the Holy Spirit is like coming up with another •“being” called God the Voice. - The Spirit and Voice belong to the only true and living God. - Not a God divisible by three. - Anything else makes the testimony a lie.

Peter relates that •“God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Spirit and with power” [Acts 10:38]. - This was the same power that enabled Christ to perform many mighty miracles during His ministry. - Likewise, Jesus worked through the apostle Paul •“in mighty signs and wonders, by the power of the Spirit of God” [Romans 15:19].

In contrast to God the Father and Jesus Christ, who are consistently compared to human beings in Their form and shape, - the Holy Spirit is consistently represented, by various symbols and manifestations, in a completely different manner—such as breath [John 20:22], - wind [Acts 2:2], - fire [Acts 2:3], - water [John 4:14; John 7:37-39], - oil [Psalms 45:7; compare Acts 10:38; Matthew 25:1-10], - a dove [Matthew 3:16] - and an “earnest,” or down payment, on eternal life [2 Corinthians 1:22; 2 Corinthians 5:5; Ephesians 1:13-14, KJV].

TO SAY THE LEAST, THESE DEPICTIONS ARE DIFFICULT TO UNDERSTAND IF THE HOLY SPIRIT IS A PERSON.


THUS, IT BEGS THE QUESTION: - If •“God” is defined as a •“personal being” with corporeal personality and Christ is the express image of that God, how then is aka •“God the Holy Spirit” [as understood by the Trinitarians] who is known as a FORMLESS •“INVISIBLE” being, devoid of corporeal personality, be also considered a “personal God”?

Nowhere does the Bible describe the Holy Spirit as having distinct bodily features as the Father, and Son, - Yet Trinitarians claim that the Holy Spirit is the "Third Being" of the Godhead. - Pantheism confounds the Spirit of God, which is really the divine power and presence of God with God Himself. - Consequently, many [trinitarians, including the current SDAs] have taken God’s Spirit-the Holy Spirit as a separate God Being altogether [aka “God the Holy Spirit”], a formless being, whose ENTIRE personage is ONLY an all pervading essence, devoid of any corporeal attributes. - This violates the Bible’s testimony of a “Personal God” and destroys the personality of God. - Furthermore, it negates the two distinct attributes of God’s personalities [corporeal and incorporeal].

Rather than describing the Holy Spirit as a distinct person or entity, the Bible most often refers to it as and connects it with God’s divine power [Zechariah 4:6; Micah 3:8].
